core_model.structure_summary: A temporal-difference (Q-learning) fixed-point iteration that updates a state-action value function toward the sum of an immediate reward and a discounted maximum over next-state values, so as to maximize an infinite-horizon discounted return, embedded in an environment whose transitions are set by a Nash-stable partition of self-interested agents maximizing utility.
core_model.canonical_form: Q(s,a)\leftarrow Q(s,a)+\alpha\big[r + \gamma\max_{a'}Q(s',a') - Q(s,a)\big];\qquad \max_{\{a\}}\sum_{t}\gamma^{t} r_t,\quad a^*=\arg\max_a Q(s,a)

variables.meaning: action-value estimate indexed by environment state and action
action (chosen scalar in a bounded interval) selected greedily to maximize the value function

notes: Two intertwined structures: (1) the Q-learning/Bellman value iteration maximizing discounted return, and (2) a game-theoretic selection layer (Nash-stable coalition partition with per-agent utility maximization) that generates the environment's dynamics/waiting times. I took the RL Bellman update as the single most central model since the proposed method (PeDP) is the Q-learning policy; the game defines the environment. max operator gives the threshold_nonlinearity; competitive_exclusion reflects agents competing for shared limited charging capacity. Could alternatively be tagged game_theoretic.
